basics.name: Jike Wang
basics.label: Ph.D. Candidate
basics.summary: My research focuses on robust, low-power sensing systems for intelligent robots and AIoT platforms operating in complex real-world environments. By combining wireless sensing, mobile robotics, and embedded intelligence, I aim to enable reliable perception and autonomy under occlusion, interference, and resource constraints. A central theme of my work is magnetic sensing for real-world autonomy, including METRO for all-weather road-marking perception, Polaris for vision-free robot pose estimation, and MagLens for mobile fine-grained imaging of hidden ferrous structures.

education.summary: Research Interests: Artificial Internet of Things (AIoT), Wireless Sensing, and Mobile Robotics. Advisor: Prof. Dongyao Chen.

projects.name: MagLens: Bringing Mobile, Fine-Grained Imaging to Ferrous Building Structures
projects.summary: Developed a mobile and low-cost magnetic imaging system for hidden ferrous structures, enabling fine-grained contour reconstruction and depth estimation.
highlights: Background: Existing non-destructive inspection tools for hidden ferrous infrastructure provide coarse detection results and cannot recover detailed geometry in a low-cost and mobile manner.
Contribution: Enabled fine-grained contour reconstruction and depth estimation with errors below 1 mm and 0.5 cm, respectively.

projects.name: Polaris: Accurate, Vision-free Fiducials for Mobile Robots with Magnetic Constellation
projects.summary: Proposed a vision-free magnetic fiducial system for robust robot pose estimation in complex environments.
highlights: Background: Traditional fiducial systems such as AprilTag are vulnerable to occlusion, energy consumption, and privacy issues.
Contribution: Achieved sub-millimeter pose estimation accuracy (<1 mm / 1°) with low-power deployment (25.08 mW).
Outcome: Published at ACM MobiCom 2024 and received Artifact Available and Functional badges.

projects.name: METRO: Magnetic Road Markings for All-weather, Smart Roads
projects.summary: Developed the first magnetic road-marking solution with long-term real-world deployment for robust all-weather road perception.
highlights: Background: Visual road surface markings degrade under rain, glare, occlusion, and wear, reducing driving reliability and safety.
Contribution: Achieved robust perception over 96% under high-speed and challenging conditions.
